title: View Runway Slot Timetable
description: The system shall display a runway slot timetable that shows all allocated slots with clear indicators for delays and emergencies.
rationale: To maintain situational awareness of runway allocations, delays, and emergencies.
business_value: Improves decision-making by providing real-time visibility into runway usage and potential issues.

source.description: As a air traffic controller I need view a runway slot timetable that shows all allocated slots with clear indicators for delays and emergencies So that maintain situational awareness

acceptance_criteria: The timetable displays all allocated slots per runway.
Delayed flights are highlighted by delay duration.
Emergency flights are marked with a special marker.
The timetable can be filtered by runway and time.
preconditions: The air traffic controller is logged into the system.
Flight data for the relevant time period is available.
postconditions: All allocated slots per runway are visible.
Delays are clearly indicated with duration.
Emergencies are marked with a special marker.
Filtering by runway and time works as expected.
functional_constraints: The system must process and display data in real-time.
Filters should return results within 2 seconds for each request.
security_requirements: All sensitive information should be encrypted during transmission and at rest.
Access to the timetable is restricted to authorized air traffic controllers only.

non_functional_requirements.performance: Response time of less than 500 milliseconds for data retrieval and filtering.
non_functional_requirements.availability: 99.9% uptime with no more than one hour of downtime per month.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system should be able to handle up to 1,000 runway slots simultaneously without performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should be clear and actionable, with a user-friendly interface for filtering.

edge_cases: What happens if there are no allocated slots for a runway?
How does the system handle flights that are both delayed and emergencies?
What if the flight data is inconsistent or incomplete?
What if the user filters by a non-existent runway?

interfaces.name: FlightDataAPI
interfaces.description: The system uses this API to fetch real-time flight data for the runway slot timetable.

interfaces.name: RunwaySlotDB
interfaces.description: The system interacts with this database to store and retrieve runway slot information.

test_cases.description: Verify the display of all allocated slots per runway.
test_cases.expected_result: All allocated slots are visible with clear labels indicating their status (normal, delayed, emergency).

test_cases.description: Check the highlighting of delayed flights.
test_cases.expected_result: Delayed flights are clearly highlighted by the duration of the delay.
Emergency flights are marked with a special marker indicating their status.
Filtering by runway returns the correct set of slots for that runway.
